_note: Ship MCP is the agent sidecar — resources for context, commands that fire events. Runtime handles lifecycle (session start/end, workspace activation, mesh registration) automatically on connect/disconnect. Agents observe and act, they don't manage.

notes: ship://project_info — project metadata, active agent, compilation status. Functional.
ship://workspaces, ship://workspaces/{branch}, ship://workspaces/{branch}/session, ship://workspaces/{branch}/provider-matrix. All read from runtime projections.
ship://sessions, ship://sessions/{workspace}. Lists session history. Reads from session projection.
ship://skills, ship://skills/{id}. Lists effective skills and reads full skill content. Should include vars in response.
ship://events, ship://events/{limit}. Recent events from event store. Depends on RT-01 event discipline.
ship://jobs, ship://jobs/{id} not yet implemented as resources. Currently only available via MCP tools.
ship://mesh not yet implemented as resource. Mesh discovery currently via tool only.
ship://session/{path}. Reads .ship-session/ files. Template-based resource. Functional.
ship://specs, ship://specs/{id}, ship://adrs, ship://adrs/{id}. Read from filesystem and DB respectively.
ship://modes, ship://providers, ship://providers/{id}/models. Config reads. Functional.
Emits session.progress event. Core command — agent signals progress or escalates blockers. Needs escalation field for human alerting.
Emits job.created event. Gated — lead/commander agents only. Event-sourced, runtime handles dispatch.
Emits job.{status} event. Agents update their own job status. Event-sourced.
Writes to .ship-session/ and fires resource notification for Studio. Thin wrapper but notification is the value.
Session auto-starts on MCP connect, drains on disconnect, resumes within grace window. Runtime-managed, not agent-commanded. Works but drain/resume needs hardening (RT-10).
Workspace detected from git branch on connect. Reconciliation runs before session start. Runtime-managed.
Agent auto-registers on mesh at connect with capabilities derived from skills. Runtime-managed.
KernelRouter actor spawned on connect with namespace subscriptions. Runtime-managed.
active_tools on agent config controls tool visibility. Empty = all. Works — commander sees 19/33 tools currently. Needs role-based defaults.
EventRelay pushes platform events to connected agent via provider-specific adapter (Claude notifications, Codex stdin). Works for Claude and Codex.
Emits workspace.completed, writes handoff.md. Questionable as agent command — workspace completion may be better as runtime-detected (all jobs done, branch merged). Needs design clarity.
Direct agent-to-agent messaging. Development/debugging tool only, not public API. Needs gating.
Broadcast to all agents. Development/debugging only, not public API. Dangerous in production.
start_session, end_session, activate_workspace, open_project, set_agent, list_*, get_* tools still exist but are superseded by auto-lifecycle and resources. Removal blocked on migration.
get_skill_vars, set_skill_var, list_skill_vars — 3 tools for skill vars is excessive. Reads should be resources. One set tool may survive.
Generic event() tool with arbitrary payload. Reserved namespaces blocked but custom events have no consumers. Needs apps model to justify. Gate behind unstable.
dispatch_agent, list_agents, stop_agent, steer_agent — unstable. Dispatch should become create_job. Direct process control is supervisor territory.
